perturbation.original_text: Unless otherwise stated herein, the Offered Shares shall also refer to the Compensation Options.
perturbation.changed_text: Unless otherwise stated herein, the Offered Shares shall not refer to the Compensation Options.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding the inclusion of Compensation Options within the definition of Offered Shares. Initially, the contract states that Offered Shares include Compensation Options, creating a broader scope. The modified text explicitly excludes Compensation Options from the definition of Offered Shares. This creates an in-text contradiction, particularly in Section 5, where the Agents' Fee is determined based on the number of 'Offered Shares sold,' potentially leading to disputes about whether Compensation Options are included in the calculation.
perturbation.location: Face page, Paragraph after gross proceeds.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration for the Agents' services hereunder, the Corporation will pay to the Agents the Agents' Fee and the Corporation shall issue to the Agents that number of Compensation Options equal to 3.5% of the number of Offered Shares sold pursuant to the Offering. Unless otherwise stated herein, the Offered Shares shall also refer to the Compensation Options. The Agents' Fee shall be apportioned among the Agents as follows: Dundee Securities Ltd. 40% Kes 7 Capital Inc. 30% Bloom Burton & Co. Ltd. 30% 100%
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly includes 'Compensation Options' within the definition of 'Offered Shares'. The changed text directly contradicts this by excluding 'Compensation Options' from the definition of 'Offered Shares'. This creates a clear and direct conflict with the contradicted text, which relies on the initial definition for calculating the Agents' Fee and issuing Compensation Options. This discrepancy would lead to disputes regarding the calculation of the Agents' Fee and the number of Compensation Options to be issued, as Section 5 explicitly mentions 'Offered Shares' in both calculations and the definition of 'Offered Shares' is altered in the Change Text. This meets the definition of Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: (aa) "Offering Price" has the meaning given to it above;
perturbation.changed_text: (aa) "Offering Price" means a price determined at the discretion of the Corporation;
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by redefining the term 'Offering Price.' Initially, the 'Offering Price' is determined $0.92 per Offered Share as stated in the initial paragraph, but the modified definition in Section 1(1)(aa) states that the 'Offering Price' is at the discretion of the Corporation. This direct contradiction will affect the agreement since the Agents are selling the offered share with a different meaning. The definition in section 1(1)(aa) will directly contradict any of the part of agreement since the term has a price fixed at the beginning of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_location: Face page, Paragraph after gross proceeds.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The undersigned, Dundee Securities Ltd. (the "Lead Agent"), Kes 7 Capital Inc., and Bloom Burton & Co. Ltd. (collectively with the Lead Agent, the "Agents" and each individually an "Agent") understand that Tribute Pharmaceuticals Canada Inc. (the "Corporation") proposes to issue and sell up to 13,043,695 common shares of the Corporation (the "Offered Shares") at a price of $0.92 per Offered Share (the "Offering Price") for aggregate gross proceeds of up to $12,000,199.40.
perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines "Offering Price" from a fixed value ($0.92 per share) to a price determined at the discretion of the Corporation. This directly contradicts the original agreement, where the agents are selling shares at a pre-determined price, as stated in the contradicted text: "at a price of $0.92 per Offered Share (the 'Offering Price')." The change introduces a clear inconsistency, making the contract unenforceable as the price is no longer fixed, aligning with the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Corporation shall pay all fees and expenses payable to its transfer agent in connection with the preparation and delivery (and, in the case of definitive certificates, execution of such definitive certificate(s) representing the Offered Shares) of the Offered Shares contemplated by this Section 7 and the fees and expenses payable to its transfer agent as may be required in the course of the distribution of the Offered Shares.
perturbation.changed_text: The Agents shall pay 50% of fees and expenses payable to the transfer agent in connection with the preparation and delivery (and, in the case of definitive certificates, execution of such definitive certificate(s) representing the Offered Shares) of the Offered Shares contemplated by this Section 7 and the fees and expenses payable to its transfer agent as may be required in the course of the distribution of the Offered Shares.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates ambiguity regarding who is responsible for paying the transfer agent's fees and expenses. The original agreement states that the Corporation is fully responsible. However, the changed text stipulates that the Agents shall pay 50% of these fees and expenses. This contradicts Section 14(a), which generally states all expenses related to the issue, sale, and delivery of Offered Shares are borne by the Corporation, including transfer agent fees. This in-text contradiction can lead to disputes over financial responsibilities.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) Whether or not the transactions contemplated by this Agreement shall be completed, all expenses of or incidental to the issue, sale and delivery of the Offered Shares and all expenses of or incidental to all other matters in connection with the offering of the Offered Shares shall be borne by the Corporation including, without limitation, all fees and disbursements of all legal counsel to the Corporation (including U.S., foreign and local counsel), all fees and disbursements of the Corporation's accountants and auditors, all expenses related to road shows and marketing activities, all printing costs incurred in connection with the offering of the Offered Shares, including certificates, if any, representing the Offered Shares, all filing fees, all fees and expenses relating to listing the Offered Shares on any exchanges, all transfer agent fees and expenses, and all reasonable out-of-pocket expenses of the Agents incurred in connection with the offering of the Offered Shares, including without limitation the fees of Canadian counsel to the Agents which shall not exceed $50,000 (not including applicable taxes and disbursements and $10,000 USD plus tax and disbursements for U.S. Counsel), and any advertising, printing, courier, telecommunications, data search, presentation, travel and other expenses incurred by the Agents together with all related taxes (including, without limitation, provincial sales taxes and HST).
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Corporation is responsible for all transfer agent fees and expenses. The changed text shifts 50% of that responsibility to the Agents. This directly contradicts Section 14(a), which states that 'all expenses of or incidental to the issue, sale and delivery of the Offered Shares...[including] all transfer agent fees and expenses, shall be borne by the Corporation'. This creates a clear and irreconcilable conflict regarding financial responsibility, which falls under the definition of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction'.
